name: Cherries
why_now: California cherry season runs through July. The window is short. Buy them when they are dark and firm and eat them without much ceremony.
how_to_choose: Dark, deep color — mahogany to near-black for sweet varieties
buy_this_much: A generous pound. They disappear faster than expected.
price_note: Still a splurge compared to most things in the basket, but July is when the price drops from its winter peak. The season is short — worth it.

flavor_paths.name: As they are
flavor_paths.description: A bowl of cherries, cold, after dinner.

flavor_paths.name: With yogurt
flavor_paths.description: Halved over Greek yogurt in the morning. That is the whole thing.

flavor_paths.name: In the drink
flavor_paths.description: Tart cherry juice becomes the Hillcrest Spritz.

weekday_uses: As the base for cherry juice in the Hillcrest Spritz
weekend_use: A plate of cherries alongside the weekend meal. No preparation needed.
storage: Refrigerate and eat within three to four days. Leave stems on until eating.
one_thing_to_learn: Tart cherry juice is a useful pantry item through July — it keeps longer than fresh cherries and makes the Hillcrest Spritz more reliable on short notice.
market_question: What variety are these, and when were they picked?
